name: automotive_platform_msgs
description: Generic Messages for Communication with an Automotive Autonomous Platform

AdaptiveCruiseControlCommand.raw:
# Adaptive Cruise Control Command Message
# Contains commands to engage/disengage ACC or adjust the set points

std_msgs/Header header

uint8 msg_counter   # Increments each time a command is sent
                    # An acknowledge message should be published with this value

float32 set_speed   # Speed setpoint (m/sec)
uint16 set          # Engage ACC at the above speed set point
uint16 resume       # Resume ACC at previous speed set point
uint16 cancel       # Disengage ACC
uint16 speed_up     # Increase speed set point
uint16 slow_down    # Decrease speed set point
uint16 further      # Increase distance set point
uint16 closer       # Decrease distance set point

fields.name: msg_counter
fields.comment: Increments each time a command is sent

fields.name: set
fields.comment: Engage ACC at the above speed set point

fields.name: resume
fields.comment: Resume ACC at previous speed set point

AdaptiveCruiseControlSettings.raw:
# Adaptive Cruise Control Settings Message
# Contains the current settings/status of ACC

std_msgs/Header header

float32 set_speed       # Current speed set point (m/sec)

uint16 following_spot   # Discrete following location
                        # 0 = following distance is the minimum allowed distance
                        # n = following distance is the maximum allowed distance
                        #      where (n + 1) is the number of allowed distances

float32 min_percent     # Minimum distance relative to maximum allowed distance
                        # 0 to 1.0

float32 step_percent    # Distance between spots relative to maximum allowed
                        # distance
                        # 0 to 1.0

float32 cipv_percent    # Where CIPV is relative to the maximum allowed distance
                        # 0 = there isn't a detected CIPV
                        # 1.0 = the CIPV is at the maximum allowed distance
                        # The maximum allowed distance varies with speed

float32 max_distance    # The distance corresponding to 1.0 percent (meters)

# So if the driver wants to maintain the greatest separation behind the CIPV,
# he can press the increase distance button over and over until the distance
# set point reaches the maximum allowed distance.  If there are 5 allowed
# following distance set points, then following_spot will equal 4.

# If a Closest In Path Vehicle is detected at this distance then the
# cipv_percent will be 1.0.

fields.name: min_percent
fields.comment: Minimum distance relative to maximum allowed distance

fields.name: step_percent
fields.comment: Distance between spots relative to maximum allowed

fields.name: cipv_percent
fields.comment: Where CIPV is relative to the maximum allowed distance

BlindSpotIndicators.raw:
# Blind Spot Indicators Active
# True if system is enabled and something is detected in the blind spot

std_msgs/Header header

bool left
bool right

BrakeCommand.raw:
# Current Brake Pedal Command

std_msgs/Header header

float32 brake_pedal     # 0 to 1.0

BrakeFeedback.raw:
# Current Brake Pedal Position Feedback

std_msgs/Header header

float32 brake_pedal     # 0 to 1.0

CabinReport.raw:
# Status of vehicle cabin sensors
std_msgs/Header header

# The door positions below are from the point of view of someone sitting
# in the vehicle, facing the X positive direction. Not using driver/passenger
# since this is different in left- vs right-hand drive vehicles.
bool door_open_front_right       # Status of the door nearest the front of the vehicle in the Y+ direction
bool door_open_front_left        # Status of the door nearest the front of the vehicle in the Y- direction
bool door_open_rear_right        # Status of the door nearest the rear of the vehicle in the Y+ direction
bool door_open_rear_left         # Status of the door nearest the rear of the vehicle in the Y- direction
bool hood_open                   # Status of the front compartment cover
bool trunk_open                  # Status of the rear compartment cover

bool passenger_present           # Whether or not a passenger is detected in the front non-driving seat
bool passenger_airbag_enabled    # Whether or not the passenger-side airbag is enabled

bool seatbelt_engaged_driver     # Whether or not the seatbelt for the driver's seat is buckled
bool seatbelt_engaged_passenger  # Whether or not the seatbelt for the passenger's seat is buckled

fields.name: door_open_front_right
fields.comment: Status of the door nearest the front of the vehicle in the Y+ direction

fields.name: door_open_front_left
fields.comment: Status of the door nearest the front of the vehicle in the Y- direction

fields.name: door_open_rear_right
fields.comment: Status of the door nearest the rear of the vehicle in the Y+ direction

fields.name: door_open_rear_left
fields.comment: Status of the door nearest the rear of the vehicle in the Y- direction

fields.name: hood_open
fields.comment: Status of the front compartment cover

fields.name: trunk_open
fields.comment: Status of the rear compartment cover

fields.name: passenger_present
fields.comment: Whether or not a passenger is detected in the front non-driving seat

fields.name: passenger_airbag_enabled
fields.comment: Whether or not the passenger-side airbag is enabled

fields.name: seatbelt_engaged_driver
fields.comment: Whether or not the seatbelt for the driver's seat is buckled

fields.name: seatbelt_engaged_passenger
fields.comment: Whether or not the seatbelt for the passenger's seat is buckled

CurvatureFeedback.raw:
# Current Curvature Feedback
# Curvature based on steering sensor feedback

std_msgs/Header header

float32 curvature           # Measured steering curvature (1/m)

DriverCommands.raw:
# Driver Commands Message
# Contains commands from the driver to the autonomy system

uint8 msg_counter   # Increments each time a command is sent
                    # An acknowledge message should be published with this value

uint16 engage       # Engage auto
uint16 disengage    # Disengage auto
uint16 speed_up     # Increase speed set point
uint16 slow_down    # Decrease speed set point
uint16 further      # Increase distance set point
uint16 closer       # Decrease distance set point
uint16 right_turn   # Right turn signal
uint16 left_turn    # Left turn signal

fields.name: msg_counter
fields.comment: Increments each time a command is sent

Gear.raw:
# Transmission Gear Value
# Provides common definitions for commanded and reported gear

uint8 NONE=0
uint8 PARK=1
uint8 REVERSE=2
uint8 NEUTRAL=3
uint8 DRIVE=4
uint8 LOW=5
uint8 gear

GearCommand.raw:
# Current Gear Command

std_msgs/Header header

automotive_platform_msgs/Gear command

GearFeedback.raw:
# Current Gear Feedback

std_msgs/Header header

automotive_platform_msgs/Gear current_gear

HillStartAssist.raw:
# Hill Start Assist Active

std_msgs/Header header

bool active

Speed.raw:
# Speed Control Message
# Contains speed commands

std_msgs/Header header

string module_name # module name of the source node of this message

float32 speed                # Desired speed (m/sec)
float32 acceleration_limit   # Max acceleration limit (m/sec^2)
float32 deceleration_limit   # Max deceleration limit (m/sec^2)

fields.name: module_name
fields.comment: module name of the source node of this message

SpeedMode.raw:
# Speed Control Message with Mode
# Contains speed commands and desired autonomy active mode flag

std_msgs/Header header

uint16 mode # Is 1 if autonomy mode should be active

float32 speed                # Desired speed (m/sec)
float32 acceleration_limit   # Max acceleration limit (m/sec^2)
float32 deceleration_limit   # Max deceleration limit (m/sec^2)

fields.name: mode
fields.comment: Is 1 if autonomy mode should be active

SpeedPedals.description: Speed Pedals Control Message with Mode
SpeedPedals.raw:
# Speed Pedals Control Message with Mode
# Contains throttle and brake commands and desired autonomy active mode flag

std_msgs/Header header

uint16 mode # Is 1 if autonomy mode should be active

float32 throttle       # 0 to 1 percent
float32 brake          # 0 to 1 percent

fields.name: mode
fields.comment: Is 1 if autonomy mode should be active

Steer.raw:
# Steering Control Message

std_msgs/Header header

string module_name # module name of the source node of this message

float32 curvature           # Desired steering curvature (1/m)
float32 max_curvature_rate  # Max rate of change for curvature (1/m/sec)

fields.name: module_name
fields.comment: module name of the source node of this message

fields.name: max_curvature_rate
fields.comment: Max rate of change for curvature (1/m/sec)

SteerMode.raw:
# Steering Control Message with Mode
# Contains steering commands and desired autonomy active mode flag

std_msgs/Header header

uint16 mode # Is 1 if autonomy mode should be active

float32 curvature           # Desired steering curvature (1/m)
float32 max_curvature_rate  # Max rate of change for curvature (1/m/sec)

fields.name: mode
fields.comment: Is 1 if autonomy mode should be active

fields.name: max_curvature_rate
fields.comment: Max rate of change for curvature (1/m/sec)

SteerWheel.description: Steering Wheel Control Message with Mode
SteerWheel.raw:
# Steering Wheel Control Message with Mode
# Contains steering wheel command and desired autonomy active mode flag

std_msgs/Header header

uint16 mode             # Is 1 if autonomy mode should be active

float32 angle           # Desired steering wheel angle (rad)
float32 angle_velocity  # Desired steering wheel angle max velocity (rad/sec)

fields.name: mode
fields.comment: Is 1 if autonomy mode should be active

fields.name: angle_velocity
fields.comment: Desired steering wheel angle max velocity (rad/sec)

SteeringCommand.raw:
# Current Steering Wheel Angle Command

std_msgs/Header header

float32 steering_wheel_angle     # Radians

SteeringFeedback.raw:
# Current Steering Wheel Angle Feedback

std_msgs/Header header

float32 steering_wheel_angle     # Radians

ThrottleCommand.raw:

std_msgs/Header header

float32 throttle_pedal     # 0 to 1.0

ThrottleFeedback.raw:
# Current Throttle Pedal Position Feedback

std_msgs/Header header

TurnSignalCommand.raw:
# Turn Signal Command with Mode
# Contains turn signal command and desired autonomy active mode flag

std_msgs/Header header

uint16 mode             # Is 1 if autonomy mode should be active

uint8 NONE=0
uint8 LEFT=1
uint8 RIGHT=2
uint8 turn_signal       # Desired turn signal on

fields.name: mode
fields.comment: Is 1 if autonomy mode should be active

UserInputADAS.description: User input (e.g. button presses) related to the factory ADAS functions
UserInputADAS.raw:
# User input (e.g. button presses) related to the factory ADAS functions
std_msgs/Header header

bool btn_cc_on          # Cruise Control on
bool btn_cc_off         # Cruise Control off
bool btn_cc_on_off      # Cruise Control on/off toggle
bool btn_cc_set_inc     # Cruise Control set/speed +
bool btn_cc_set_dec     # Cruise Control set/speed -
bool btn_cc_res         # Cruise Control resume
bool btn_cc_cncl        # Cruise Control cancel
bool btn_cc_res_cncl    # Cruise Control resume/cancle toggle
bool btn_acc_gap_inc    # Adaptive Cruise Control gap -
bool btn_acc_gap_dec    # Adaptive Cruise Control gap +
bool btn_lka_on         # Lane Keep Assist off
bool btn_lka_off        # Lane Keep Assist on
bool btn_lka_on_off     # Lane Keep Assist on/off toggle
